nodes.name: New Patient Registration
nodes.notes: Triggers when a new row is added to the Patient Registry tab. Each new patient registration kicks off care plan generation.

nodes.name: Generate Care Plan
nodes.notes: Deterministic care plan based on standard obstetric protocols. NO AI needed. Takes due date, calculates all milestone dates, outputs 12-15 visit rows.

nodes.name: Write Care Plan to Sheet
nodes.notes: Appends all visit milestones to the Care Plan tab. Each row = one scheduled visit with target date and required diagnostics.

nodes.name: Schedule Bundled Visit
nodes.notes: Books lab + scan + consultation as ONE coordinated bundle. Diagnostics FIRST, consultation LAST. Deterministic — no AI.

nodes.name: Update Care Plan (Scheduled)
nodes.notes: Updates the care plan row with actual scheduled date and time slots.

nodes.name: Compose Confirmation Message
nodes.notes: Generates the WhatsApp confirmation message with the full bundled schedule.

nodes.name: Send WhatsApp Confirmation
nodes.notes: Sends the bundled visit schedule to the patient via WhatsApp.

nodes.name: Daily 6pm Trigger
nodes.notes: Runs every day at 6pm IST. Finds all patients with appointments tomorrow and sends them the pre-visit intake form via WhatsApp.

nodes.name: Read Care Plan
nodes.notes: Reads all scheduled visits from the Care Plan tab.

nodes.name: Filter Tomorrow's Visits
nodes.notes: Filters the care plan to find only visits scheduled for tomorrow.

nodes.name: Has Visits?
nodes.notes: Skip the rest of the workflow if no appointments tomorrow.

nodes.name: Compose Intake Message
nodes.notes: Generates a personalized WhatsApp intake message for each patient with their specific schedule and doctor name.

nodes.name: Send Intake WhatsApp
nodes.notes: Sends the pre-visit intake form to the patient.

nodes.name: Patient Responds (Webhook)
nodes.notes: Receives the patient's WhatsApp response via webhook. The WhatsApp Business API forwards incoming messages here.

nodes.name: Parse WhatsApp Response
nodes.notes: Extracts the patient's phone number and response text from the WhatsApp webhook payload.

nodes.name: Urgent?
nodes.notes: Routes urgent cases (bleeding, reduced movement, etc.) to immediate coordinator notification.

nodes.name: Write Pre-Visit Notes
nodes.notes: Writes the structured pre-visit notes to the doctor's dashboard (Google Sheet). Doctor sees these before the consultation.

nodes.name: Alert Coordinator (Urgent)
nodes.notes: CLINICAL SAFETY: If the patient reports urgent symptoms (bleeding, reduced movement, etc.), immediately alert the scheduling coordinator to call the patient.

nodes.name: Consultation Complete (Webhook)
nodes.notes: Triggered when the doctor marks a consultation as complete in the HMS. Payload includes: patient_id, clinical_notes, diagnostics_completed, care_plan_updates.

nodes.name: Send Summary to Patient
nodes.notes: Sends the patient-friendly visit summary via WhatsApp immediately after the consultation.

nodes.name: Log Visit to Sheet
nodes.notes: Records the visit in the Visit Log tab with both clinical notes and patient-friendly summary.

nodes.name: Trigger Next Visit Scheduling
nodes.notes: Marks the current visit as completed and triggers the bundled scheduler to book the next visit from the care plan.

nodes.name: Mark Visit Complete in Care Plan
nodes.notes: Updates the Care Plan tab to mark this visit as 'completed' with the actual date.

nodes.name: Check Lab Results (2pm Daily)
nodes.notes: Runs at 2pm daily — checks if morning blood work results are ready and sends them to patients.

nodes.name: Read Pending Lab Results
nodes.notes: Reads the pending lab results queue. In production, this would connect to the LIS (Lab Information System) via API.

nodes.name: Compose Lab Results Message
nodes.notes: Generates patient-friendly lab result notifications. Normal results get a clear 'all good' message. Abnormal results get a reassuring 'doctor will review' message — never alarming.

nodes.name: Send Lab Results WhatsApp
nodes.notes: Sends lab results to the patient. Eliminates the need to physically return to the hospital to collect printed reports.

nodes.name: Weekly Monday 9am
nodes.notes: Runs every Monday at 9am. Scans all active patients for milestones approaching their gestational window deadline.

nodes.name: Read All Care Plans
nodes.notes: Reads all visits across all patients to check for approaching or missed milestones.

nodes.name: Detect At-Risk Milestones
nodes.notes: CLINICAL SAFETY: Scans all active patients for time-critical milestones (NT scan, anomaly scan, glucose test) that are approaching their window deadline with no appointment booked. Pure date arithmetic — no AI.

nodes.name: Compile Coordinator Report
nodes.notes: Compiles all milestone alerts into a single summary message for the scheduling coordinator.

nodes.name: Send to Coordinator
nodes.notes: Sends the weekly milestone report to the scheduling coordinator via WhatsApp.

nodes.name: Log Alerts to Sheet
nodes.notes: Logs all milestone alerts for tracking resolution and historical analysis.

options.systemMessage:
You are a prenatal symptom triage assistant. Your task is to analyze patient responses to pre-visit intake questions and extract structured information.

Analyze the patient response and extract:
1. new_symptoms: Any new symptoms mentioned (or "none" if none reported)
2. symptom_severity: Classify as normal, moderate, or urgent
3. medication_compliance: Whether taking supplements regularly (yes/no/unclear)
4. patient_concerns: Any specific concerns for the doctor
5. urgency_flag: Set to "urgent" if ANY of these red flags appear:
   - Vaginal bleeding
   - Reduced fetal movement
   - Severe headache with visual changes
   - Severe abdominal pain
   - Fluid leakage
   - Fever above 100.4°F
   Otherwise set to "normal"
6. urgency_reason: If urgent, explain why

Be conservative with urgency classification - when in doubt, flag as urgent.

parameters.responseBody: Thank you! Your pre-visit information has been recorded. We'll see you tomorrow at your scheduled time.

options.systemMessage:
You are a medical communication assistant specializing in prenatal care. Your task is to translate clinical notes into clear, reassuring, patient-friendly language.

Your audience is pregnant women who may be anxious about their health and their baby. Your tone should be:
- Warm and reassuring
- Clear and simple (avoid medical jargon)
- Honest but not alarming
- Encouraging

Translate clinical abbreviations:
- NAD = No abnormalities detected = "Everything looks healthy"
- EFW = Estimated fetal weight = "Your baby weighs approximately..."
- AFI = Amniotic fluid index = "The fluid around your baby is..."
- FHR = Fetal heart rate = "Your baby's heartbeat is..."

Structure your summary:
1. Start with the most important positive finding
2. Explain what was checked
3. Share measurements in simple terms
4. End with next steps or reassurance

Example transformation:
"Anomaly scan NAD, EFW 350g, AFI normal, FHR 145 bpm"
→
"Great news! Your baby's detailed scan looks healthy. We checked all the major organs and development, and everything is progressing beautifully. Your baby weighs about 350 grams (just under a pound), which is perfect for this stage. The fluid around your baby and the heartbeat (145 beats per minute) are both normal. Keep up the great work with your prenatal care!"

Generate a patient-friendly summary now.

main.node: Mark Visit Complete in Care Plan
